title: After a critique from Rubio, WHO defends work on Ebola response
description: The WHO defended its Ebola response, noting it's national authorities who detect outbreaks. Marco Rubio had said the WHO was "a little late."

title: WHO says Ebola not a "pandemic emergency," and U.S. criticism over response may be down to "misunderstanding" - CBS News

title: Ebola death toll rises as two Americans are flown to Europe for monitoring
description: The suspected death toll from the Ebola outbreak in central Africa rose again Wednesday as a group of potentially exposed Americans was arriving in Europe for monitoring and the World Health Organization warned a vaccine was still months away.

title: WHO says risk of global spread of Ebola outbreak is low, but high at national, regional levels

title: Transcript - Updates on CDC's Response to Ebola Outbreak in DRC and Uganda and Hantavirus Linked to M/V Hondius, 5/19/2026
description: Transcript - Updates on CDCs Response to Ebola Outbreak in DRC and Uganda and Hantavirus Linked to M/V Hondius, 5/19/2026

title: CDC Orders Quarantine for Two MV Hondius Passengers at UNMC
description: AP reports that CDC issued federal quarantine orders for two MV Hondius passengers now at the University of Nebraska Medical Center. The report says 18 people from the ship are being assessed at UNMC, with the other 16 asked to remain at the facility through May 31.

title: CDC Provides Update on Hantavirus Outbreak Linked to M/V Hondius Cruise Ship
description: CDC Provides Update on Hantavirus Outbreak Linked to M/V Hondius Cruise Ship

title: ECDC Updates Andes Hantavirus Outbreak Surveillance Page
description: ECDC's May 19 surveillance update lists 9 confirmed cases, 2 probable cases, 0 suspected cases, and 3 deaths linked to the MV Hondius Andes hantavirus outbreak. ECDC states that additional cases may still be identified because of the long incubation period, while the risk to the EU/EEA general population remains very low.

title: Canadian from hantavirus-hit cruise ship tests positive
description: The individual is one of four former passengers on the MV Hondius isolating on Vancouver Island, British Columbia.

title: PHAC Confirms Canada's First Andes Hantavirus Case Linked to MV Hondius
description: The Public Health Agency of Canada confirmed through National Microbiology Laboratory testing that one MV Hondius passenger in Canada tested positive for Andes hantavirus. A travelling partner tested negative, no further Canadian cases had been identified at the time of the update, and high-risk contacts remained in isolation and monitoring.

title: WHO Publishes Global Rapid Risk Assessment v2 for Andes Hantavirus
description: WHO published version 2 of its rapid risk assessment for the Andes hantavirus outbreak associated with MV Hondius on May 17. The assessment addresses public health impact, risk of geographic spread, and response-capacity considerations for national authorities and international partners.

title: Colorado Man Dies of Hantavirus — Sin Nombre Virus, NOT Related to MV Hondius Cruise Ship Outbreak
description: IMPORTANT CLINICAL DISTINCTION: A Colorado man has died of hantavirus pulmonary syndrome caused by Sin Nombre virus (SNV) — the rodent-borne strain endemic to the western United States. This case is completely unrelated to the MV Hondius Andes virus outbreak. Sin Nombre virus is transmitted by deer mice via inhalation of infected rodent droppings; it does NOT transmit person-to-person. The CDC and Colorado CDPHE confirm no epidemiological link to the cruise ship cluster. This is a separate endemic exposure in a rural outdoor setting.

title: Emory University Hospital Releases Both Hantavirus-Monitored Passengers
description: Emory University Hospital in Atlanta has released both U.S. repatriation passengers who had been under monitoring following the MV Hondius Andes hantavirus outbreak. Neither patient tested positive for hantavirus. Both were cleared after completing the required observation period. This leaves UNMC in Nebraska as the primary U.S. monitoring facility for the remaining repatriated passengers.

title: France Confirms Andes Virus Genomic Match to Known Strain — Consistent with Cruise Ship Origin
description: French public health authorities have confirmed genomic sequencing of the French passenger's Andes virus isolate matches the near-identical cluster sequences published by Switzerland, Netherlands, and WHO — consistent with a single zoonotic spillover event aboard MV Hondius. The French genome has been submitted to international databases. This further strengthens the single-source hypothesis and rules out independent community exposure in France.

title: CDC Updates Home Monitoring Guidance for MV Hondius Andes Virus Contacts
description: CDC has updated and tightened its home monitoring guidance for individuals who had potential exposure to Andes hantavirus through the MV Hondius outbreak. Updated guidance clarifies symptom monitoring thresholds, mandatory check-in frequency with state health departments, and expanded criteria for escalation to medical evaluation. The update reflects lessons learned from the first weeks of monitoring approximately 41 Americans across 11 states.

title: WHO Revises Case Count as U.S. Passenger Tests Negative; Canadian Case Brings New Country Total to 9
description: Reuters reports that the U.S. passenger who had inconclusive hantavirus test results (discordant results from two labs) has tested definitively NEGATIVE following retesting at CDC laboratories. This removes the U.S. from the active case roster. Separately, a new confirmed case has emerged in Canada (British Columbia), maintaining or increasing the total case count. The revised global case total and country count will be reflected in the next WHO Disease Outbreak Notice.

title: WHO Convenes Emergency Scientific Consultation on Andes Virus Medical Countermeasures
description: WHO convened an emergency scientific consultation on May 15 to assess available and investigational medical countermeasures for Andes hantavirus, including candidate antiviral therapies, monoclonal antibody treatments, and experimental vaccine platforms. The consultation included review of favipiravir data, the Jay Hooper DNA vaccine (phase 1 completed at USAMRIID), and ribavirin data. WHO noted significant gaps in approved therapeutics and called for accelerated research coordination.

title: WHO Director-General Press Conference: Andes Hantavirus Global Risk Assessment Remains LOW
description: WHO Director-General Dr. Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus held a press conference on May 15 reaffirming the global risk assessment as LOW for the general population. He noted the outbreak remains geographically linked to a single vessel, transmission chains are being effectively contained, and no community transmission has been identified. He called for increased research investment in hantavirus countermeasures and praised international coordination efforts.

title: Doubts Grow Over Theory That Bird Watchers Trip to Argentine Landfill Sparked Hantavirus Outbreak
description: The leading hypothesis for the MV Hondius outbreak's index case — a birdwatcher potentially exposed to rodent-infected material at an Argentine landfill — is facing mounting scrutiny. Investigators examining genomic and epidemiological evidence report difficulty confirming a clear chain of transmission from this proposed source event, raising questions about whether the true spillover site and index case have been correctly identified.

title: Questions Swirl About U.S., Michigan's Ability to Quell Hantavirus
description: Detroit Free Press (May 14): 41 Americans now under 42-day monitoring. Michigan's state laboratory cannot run hantavirus serology tests; some states lack lab capacity entirely. CDC PCR tests exist but are not licensed for patient use — only serology available domestically. Public health experts raise concerns about systemic preparedness gaps amid CDC and NIH funding cuts.

title: Hantavirus Treatments Are Coming, But Funding Is Holding Them Back
description: No approved antiviral or vaccine exists for hantavirus. Scientific American reviews the treatment pipeline — candidate neutralizing antibody therapies, DNA and mRNA vaccine platforms, and supportive care advances — and examines how chronic funding gaps, limited commercial incentive, and research infrastructure cuts are delaying clinical advancement of promising candidates at precisely the moment a global outbreak has created renewed urgency.

title: CDC Doesn't Have Hantavirus PCR Test as 10 People In US Being Monitored
description: Newsweek (May 13): 10 people across five U.S. states (California, Kansas, Maryland, New Jersey, Washington) under monitoring after exposure to MV Hondius passengers. Kansas reports three individuals exposed through contact with a confirmed case. None symptomatic; all states report low local risk. CDC PCR test exists but remains in research stage and cannot be used for patient management per HHS.

title: Disease Outbreak News DON601: Hantavirus Cluster — Cruise Ship Update
description: WHO DON601 (May 13): 11 total cases (8 confirmed, 2 probable, 1 inconclusive), 3 deaths, CFR 27%. New confirmed cases in France and Spain. One U.S. case inconclusive — discordant results from two laboratories, retesting underway. CDC states no confirmed U.S. cases. Genomic sequencing indicates near-identical sequences across cases, consistent with single zoonotic spillover and subsequent human-to-human transmission.

title: How to Arm Yourself Against Hantavirus Misinformation
description: Scientific American identifies and debunks prevalent misinformation circulating on social media about the 2026 Andes hantavirus outbreak, including false claims about laboratory origin, transmission routes, and treatment options. The guide provides evidence-based framing drawn from WHO, CDC, and ECDC assessments, with practical guidance on evaluating health information sources during a rapidly evolving outbreak.

title: Why Hantavirus Takes So Long to Show Symptoms — And What That Means for Containment
description: Andes hantavirus has an unusually long incubation period of approximately 9–33 days, far longer than most respiratory pathogens. Scientific American explains the biological basis for this delay, how the MV Hondius timeline confirms the upper range of these estimates, and the practical challenges it creates for contact tracing, quarantine duration decisions, and early clinical recognition by providers unfamiliar with hantavirus disease.

title: Disease Outbreak News: Hantavirus Disease Associated with a Cruise Ship — Update
description: WHO reports expanded case count and geographic spread in the MV Hondius cluster. International investigations ongoing.

title: How Hantavirus Actually Spreads Is a Key Question
description: Harvard T.H. Chan School of Public Health examines the critical unresolved question at the center of the MV Hondius investigation: distinguishing primary rodent-to-human spillover from secondary person-to-person spread. The piece explores what close-contact exposures aboard a contained vessel reveal about Andes virus transmission thresholds, and the implications for infection control guidance and quarantine policy as cases spread to multiple countries.

title: Hantavirus Conspiracy Theories Are Already Spreading Online
description: Wired reports on misinformation and conspiracy theories circulating on social media about the 2026 Andes hantavirus outbreak, tracking the spread of false claims. (Publication date approximate — source site blocked automated retrieval.)

title: Andes Hantavirus Cruise Ship Outbreak
description: Science News covers the 2026 Andes hantavirus outbreak aboard MV Hondius, tracing the ship's voyage from Ushuaia, Argentina through the South Atlantic and the emergence of cases among passengers and crew. Examines what makes Andes virus (Orthohantavirus andesense) uniquely dangerous among hantaviruses — including its documented capacity for person-to-person transmission — and what the outbreak reveals about surveillance and response gaps for rare hemorrhagic fevers.

title: CDC Reveals It Doesn't Have a Hantavirus PCR Test Yet
description: Report that the CDC has disclosed it does not yet have a validated PCR diagnostic test for hantavirus, raising questions about US diagnostic capacity during the ongoing Andes virus outbreak. (Publication date approximate — source site blocked automated retrieval.)

title: The Race to Develop an Andes Hantavirus Test
description: Wired coverage of the diagnostic gap in the 2026 Andes virus outbreak and the race among laboratories and public health agencies to develop and validate rapid PCR and serological tests. (Publication date approximate — source site blocked automated retrieval.)

title: There Is No Vaccine for Deadly Hantavirus, But This Scientist Is Working on One
description: Jay Hooper (US Army Medical Research Institute of Infectious Diseases) has spent 30+ years developing hantavirus vaccines. His team has completed phase 1 trials for an Andes virus DNA vaccine that induces neutralizing antibodies. Progress is limited by funding constraints and insufficient commercial incentive; mRNA platforms are also being explored.

title: Hantavirus: How It's Different From COVID-19 And The US Response
description: Forbes analysis of the 2026 Andes virus outbreak examining key differences from COVID-19 and the current US public health response posture.

title: HAN 528: Andes Virus Infection Associated with Cruise Ship Voyage
description: CDC Health Alert Network advisory for U.S. clinicians and public health professionals on the MV Hondius Andes virus cluster.

title: Disease Outbreak News: Hantavirus Disease Associated with a Cruise Ship
description: WHO initial DON confirming Andes virus as causative agent. 11 confirmed or probable cases, 3 deaths, 8 countries reporting exposure.
